error.signature: npm ERR! ENOENT — npm install fails in directory with Korean or CJK characters
error.regex: (npm ERR!.*ENOENT.*[가-힣]|ENOENT.*[\\u]|npm.*non-ascii.*path|npm\ ERR!\ ENOENT\ —\ npm\ install\ fails\ in\ directory\ with\ Korean)

verdict.summary: npm install fails or produces corrupted node_modules when the project directory contains Korean/CJK characters in its path.

dead_ends.why_fails: Path encoding handling is an OS/filesystem issue, not npm-version-specific
Flag is for dependency resolution conflicts, not path encoding issues

workarounds.action: Move project to an ASCII-only path or create a symlink from an ASCII path
Set system locale to UTF-8 and use Node.js with --icu-data-dir for full ICU support
workarounds.how: export LANG=en_US.UTF-8; export LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8; npm install
